behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names Context.id, module, reloader.demo_name, demo.blocks, gradio utility module, and the exec/sys.modules sequence — all items the tests assert on.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what's broken and the required post-condition (Context.id advanced past all existing block IDs) without specifying the exact code (e.g., max(demo.blocks.keys())+1 formula is implied by the requirement but not dictated as code).

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check behavior via mocks on Context.id values; alternative implementations (using max, or iterating keys, or using len) would all satisfy the assertions.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests extract the code region and exec it with mocks, plus run pytest suites — behavior is executed not text-matched.

test_not_tautological.reason: f2p tests assert Context.id increases to specific thresholds (>=11, >=101, >=43), which a no-op stub would fail.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover the Context.id bumping, edge cases (empty blocks, missing demo, None demo), and pass_to_pass (lint, format, reload tests).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are divided into Gates and Fail-to-pass sections with comments and descriptive names.

no_typos.reason: Paths, names, and commands are consistent.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction mentions 'Gradio utility module' and the exec/sys.modules sequence but doesn't give line numbers; localization is minimal and needed for the task.
